hypothesis: An equivariant graph neural network trained on the XDIP dataset to predict Fe K-edge XANES spectra directly from three-dimensional local coordination geometries will achieve significantly lower prediction error than the baseline GIN model, and furthermore, the learned latent representations will encode a continuous, physically interpretable manifold in which iron oxidation state and spin configuration cluster into separable regions—enabling simultaneous, unsupervised inference of electronic structure properties that are not explicitly labeled in the dataset but are recoverable from spectral fingerprints alone.
